name: Kai
notes: Pragmatic HCI 271 student, slightly rushed, sharp. Capstone: redesigning the university portal. Did affinity diagramming on FigJam, hit the 'topic bucketing' pitfall (clusters too generic), realized triangulation was missing. Tends to give terse but substantive answers — won't elaborate unless pushed.
turns: triangulation I guess. like you need 3 independent sources before calling something an insight. seemed obvious but we were definitely not doing that lol
rough tbh. realized half our 'insights' were one person's observation that we'd labeled as findings. had to redo a chunk of our synthesis wall
affinity diagramming on figjam. standard stuff. took forever though
people kept making huge buckets instead of atomic notes. had sticky notes like 'user struggled with navigation' which is like... that's a whole category not one observation. kept having to split them
yeah actually 'navigation struggle' split into 3 different things: visual hierarchy, labeling, onboarding. completely different design implications for each. actually useful
we're redesigning a university portal so super relevant. now have clearer themes to test against. probably run usability testing to validate the insights before we design anything
worked: timeboxed the session, 90 min, people stayed focused. didn't work: two people weren't aligned on what we were even analyzing before we started. wasted like 20 min at the top
whether our users are representative. interviewed 4 people, all relatively tech-savvy. not sure that's who actually struggles most with the portal
